title: campanula glomerata
intro: Campanula glomerata, also known as the cluster bellflower, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its blue-violet flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, where it is valued for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Campanula glomerata are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as reduction of inflammation and pain. Campanula glomerata is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use, although its preparation and application should be done under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
